data.title: XtalPi Flags Interim Loss as R&D Push Weighs on Earnings
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The latest announcement is out from QuantumPharm, Inc. ( (HK:2228) ).

XtalPi Holdings Limited has warned investors that it expects to swing to a net loss of about RMB215 million to RMB275 million for the six months ended June 30, 2026, compared with a net profit of roughly RMB82.8 million a year earlier. The company also anticipates a decline in headline revenue to around RMB380 million to RMB400 million, largely due to a high comparison base created by a major pipeline licensing upfront payment booked in the prior period.

Excluding that one-off licensing effect, XtalPi projects at least RMB250 million in revenue, more than 65% higher year-on-year, with its AI for Science intelligent solutions set to grow over 120% to no less than RMB180 million. The group attributes the loss to a more than 50% increase in research and development spending to at least RMB340 million, as it accelerates investment in autonomous labs, agentic systems and diversified drug technology platforms, a strategy it believes will reinforce long-term R&D strength and sustain future revenue growth.

More about QuantumPharm, Inc.

XtalPi Holdings Limited operates in the AI-driven drug discovery and scientific research sector, providing intelligent solutions, robotic laboratory systems and related services to pharmaceutical and biotech clients. The group focuses on Scientific AI, Physical AI and agentic systems, and is expanding into proprietary drug pipelines and multimodal technology platforms spanning small and large molecules, molecular glues, peptides and oligonucleotides.

The company is pursuing a strategic shift from pure service provision toward building asset-based platforms and pipelines with long-term commercialization potential. It has launched XtalPi Science, an open intelligent R&D platform aimed at scientific research scenarios, and is investing heavily in autonomous laboratories and integrated AI systems to create a closed-loop research and development ecosystem.

Average Trading Volume: 58,873,850

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$30.11B

data.title: Majestic Dragon Aerotech Calls 2026 AGM and Seeks Fresh Share Issuance Mandate
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

State Energy Group International Assets Holdings Limited ( (HK:0918) ) has issued an update.

Majestic Dragon Aerotech Holdings has called its annual general meeting for 18 August 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review audited financial statements for the year ended 31 March 2026 and consider re-election of key board members, including one executive and two independent non-executive directors. Investors will also vote on reappointing Global Link CPA Limited as auditor and granting directors a general mandate to issue up to 20% of the company’s share capital, providing flexibility for future share issuance and potential capital-raising activities.

The proposed general mandate would allow the board to allot, issue and deal with additional shares, including securities convertible into shares, subject to specified limits and exclusions such as rights issues and share option exercises. This authority, if approved, is time-limited to the next AGM cycle and is intended to enhance the company’s financial and strategic agility, potentially affecting shareholder dilution, governance oversight, and the company’s ability to respond quickly to market opportunities.

More about State Energy Group International Assets Holdings Limited

Majestic Dragon Aerotech Holdings Limited, incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong under stock code 918, operates in the broader aviation and aerotech sector. The company is overseen by a board comprising executive and independent non-executive directors, and it maintains its corporate governance and reporting in line with Hong Kong listing requirements.

data.title: Majestic Dragon Aerotech Overhauls Bye-Laws to Align with HK Listing Reforms
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

State Energy Group International Assets Holdings Limited ( (HK:0918) ) has shared an announcement.

Majestic Dragon Aerotech Holdings Limited plans to overhaul its constitutional documents by replacing its existing amended and restated bye-laws with a new set that aligns more closely with updated Hong Kong listing regulations. The proposed changes aim to accommodate the uncertificated securities market regime, enable the holding of repurchased shares as treasury stock, and support hybrid meetings, electronic voting, and broader use of paperless corporate communications.

The board will seek shareholder approval for the new bye-laws by special resolution at the annual general meeting scheduled for 18 August 2026, with a circular detailing the amendments to be dispatched in due course. If approved, the move is expected to modernise the company’s governance framework, enhance operational flexibility in capital management, and bring its corporate procedures in line with evolving market infrastructure and regulatory expectations in Hong Kong.

More about State Energy Group International Assets Holdings Limited

Majestic Dragon Aerotech Holdings Limited, incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong under stock code 918, operates within the broader aviation and aerotech sector. The company is overseen by a board comprising executive and independent non-executive directors and is subject to the regulatory framework of the Hong Kong capital markets.

MeiG Smart Technology Co., Ltd., a Shenzhen-based smart technology group listed in Hong Kong and on the Shenzhen Stock Exchange, is led by chairman Wang Ping and a mix of executive and independent non-executive directors. MeiG Smart Technology has issued a supplemental announcement on its previously disclosed acquisition of 100% equity in a target company, which fully owns a target property, via its wholly owned subsidiary ZhongGe Smart. Auntea Jenny (Shanghai) Industrial Co., Ltd. has issued a positive profit alert for the six months ended June 30, 2026, projecting profit of RMB304 million to RMB325 million, up about 50% to 60% from a year earlier, with adjusted profit expected to rise 36% to 46%. The company attributes the strong interim performance mainly to its multi-brand strategy, expansion of its store network, successful new product launches and refined operational management, while cautioning that the figures are preliminary and unaudited and advising investors to exercise care when trading its shares.

The forecasted jump in earnings underscores Auntea Jenny’s strengthening market position in its segment, as economies of scale from business expansion and disciplined operations appear to be translating into higher margins. For shareholders and potential investors, the alert signals robust underlying business momentum ahead of the formal interim results release due by end-July 2026, though management stresses that non-IFRS adjusted metrics should be used alongside standard measures and that final results may differ after audit and committee review.

data.title: Qilu Expressway Posts Higher Revenue but Softer Profit on Toll Traffic Diversions
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Qilu Expressway Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1576) ) has provided an announcement.

Qilu Expressway Company Limited, a PRC-incorporated toll-road operator listed in Hong Kong, generates the bulk of its turnover from toll income on expressways in Shandong. Its asset-heavy balance sheet and reliance on regional traffic patterns underscore its role as a long-term infrastructure provider in China’s transport sector.

For the six months ended 30 June 2026, the Group reported revenue of about RMB1,113 million, up 4.25% year-on-year, while net profit slipped 1.79% to roughly RMB195 million as toll income fell 4.12% amid traffic diversion and construction-related closures.

Total non-current assets edged down 1.42% and equity declined 1.06% over the period, while current assets rose 14.8%, indicating some strengthening of short-term liquidity even as profitability softened due to operational headwinds on key road sections.

More about Qilu Expressway Co., Ltd. Class H

Qilu Expressway Company Limited is a joint stock company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China that operates toll expressways, focusing on road transportation infrastructure in Shandong province. The Group derives most of its revenue from toll income, positioning it as a regional player in China’s expressway and toll-road sector.

Its business performance is closely tied to traffic volumes, the configuration of surrounding road networks and construction activity, making it sensitive to diversion and closure effects on key routes.

The company’s capital structure includes substantial non-current assets and liabilities typical of long-term infrastructure operations, with equity reflecting its accumulated investments and earnings in the toll-road segment.

data.description:

AsiaInfo Technologies Limited ( (HK:1675) ) has provided an update.

AsiaInfo Technologies warned that revenue for the first half of 2026 is expected to fall to about RMB2.10 billion–RMB2.30 billion from RMB2.598 billion a year earlier, with net loss widening to roughly RMB460 million–RMB490 million. The downturn stems from weaker telecom core system demand, deliberate cuts to low-quality ICT and labor-intensive projects, and one-off severance costs from workforce restructuring.

Excluding restructuring charges, the group still anticipates a larger underlying loss of RMB240 million–RMB270 million as it reshapes its portfolio. Management says the short-term hit reflects an “AI First” strategy, with faster growth in smart digital operations, progress toward commercialising its Physical AI products for manufacturers, and deployment of a full-stack satellite internet platform integrated into China’s low-Earth orbit constellation, positioning the company for longer-term AI-era growth.

AsiaInfo Technologies Limited is a Hong Kong–listed IT solutions provider focused on telecom core systems, smart digital operations and emerging AI-driven businesses. The group serves telecommunications operators, government and enterprise clients, and is increasingly targeting AI-enabled applications, physical manufacturing and satellite internet infrastructure in China.

Luk Fook Holdings (International) Limited has convened its next annual general meeting for 20 August 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review the audited consolidated financial statements and directors’ and auditor’s reports for the year ended 31 March 2026. ISP Holdings Limited ( (HK:2340) ) has provided an announcement.

ISP Holdings’ construction subsidiary ISP Construction (Engineering) Limited has secured a partial court win in a major contractual dispute with ATAL Engineering Limited over an outstanding claim of about HK$98.5 million. The High Court of Hong Kong ruled that ATAL repudiated the contract, confirmed ISPCE’s entitlement to an acceleration claim, dismissed ATAL’s counterclaim, but found ISPCE had not proven prolongation costs.

The judgment covers only the first phase of a bifurcated trial, with the second phase in August 2026 to decide the quantum of sums recoverable, leaving the financial impact on ISP Holdings still uncertain. The company has urged shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution as the eventual monetary award could materially affect its financial position and will provide further updates as required under Hong Kong listing rules.

More about ISP Holdings Limited

ISP Holdings Limited, incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong, operates through subsidiaries including ISP Construction (Engineering) Limited, which is engaged in construction-related engineering services. The group focuses on project-based contracts in Hong Kong, exposing it to complex contractual and legal disputes typical of the construction industry.

ISP Holdings Limited ( (HK:2340) ) has shared an update.

The move represents an administrative update to the company’s corporate registration arrangements, with no indication of changes to its business operations or strategic direction, but remains relevant for regulatory, legal and shareholder correspondence purposes.

The board composition, reaffirmed in the announcement, shows Kingston Chu Chun Ho serving as chairman and executive director alongside a non-executive director and three independent non-executive directors. data.title: COFCO Joycome warns of heavy first-half loss on weak hog prices
data.description:

COFCO Joycome Foods Limited ( (HK:1610) ) has provided an update.

COFCO Joycome Foods Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates in the agri-food sector with a focus on hog production, branded meat products and feed manufacturing. The group emphasizes end-to-end value chain management, including breeding R&D, procurement, production and health management, while expanding its branded and feed businesses through product innovation and wider sales channels.

The company warned that it expects a loss of about RMB1.0 billion to RMB1.2 billion for the first half of 2026, compared with a profit of RMB198 million a year earlier. Management attributed the swing to lower hog selling prices squeezing margins, noting that cost reductions, hedging with hog futures and growth in branded and feed businesses were insufficient to offset the price pressure.

The announcement underscores the vulnerability of its hog production segment to market price cycles, even as operational efficiencies and diversification efforts are strengthened. Shareholders and potential investors have been cautioned ahead of the formal interim results release due in late August, highlighting heightened earnings risk for stakeholders in the near term.

More about COFCO Joycome Foods Limited

COFCO Joycome Foods Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates in the agri-food sector with a focus on hog production, branded meat products and feed manufacturing. The group emphasizes end-to-end value chain management, including breeding R&D, procurement, production and health management, while expanding its branded and feed businesses through product innovation and wider sales channels.

The company has been building its market presence by investing in new product development, brand building and channel expansion to drive growth in its branded meat operations. Its feed business remains oriented toward product innovation and market expansion, delivering year-on-year growth in business scale despite volatility in upstream hog prices.
